title: Signals and Systems - Problems
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems for Chapter 1 (Signals and Systems), focusing on signal energy and power calculations. Problems cover finding energies of various signals, effects of sign change, time shifting, scaling, and periodicity on energy and power. A practical problem involves designing a 'soft-start' pulse with equivalent energy to a square pulse.

figures.caption: Various continuous-time signals including triangular waveforms x(t), x₁(t), x₂(t), x₃(t), and x₄(t) used for signal energy and property analysis exercises.
figures.description: The figure labeled Figure P1.1-2 shows five continuous-time signals on horizontal time axes t and vertical amplitude axes, titled x(t), x₁(t), x₂(t), x₃(t), and x₄(t). Each plot is a finite-duration ramp/triangular segment: x(t) rises from 0 at t=0 to 1 at t=1, x₁(t) falls from 1 at t=-1 to 0 at t=0, x₂(t) falls from 0 at t=0 to -1 at t=1, x₃(t) rises from 0 at t=1 to 1 at t=2, and x₄(t) rises from 0 at t=0 to 2 at t=1, with vertical edges marking start or end points. Students should notice how these signals are related by time shift, time reversal, sign inversion, and amplitude scaling.
